summary: Full-stack testing of GH05T3 MVP. 9/9 backend pytest pass after fixing one 500 error. Frontend loads, chat flow works with Claude Sonnet 4.5 responses, Kairos/Nightly/PCL buttons all trigger backend updates.

minor.issue: Returned 500 because MongoDB mutated the dict in-place adding ObjectId `_id` which FastAPI couldn't serialize. FIXED by popping _id before returning.

issues: query_selector_all('[data-testid^="pcl-"]') returned 8 buttons — expected 7 PCL palette states. Verify no extra stray data-testid starting with 'pcl-' (likely just a container). Not blocking — first palette click worked and PCL halo updated.

action_items: Optionally audit data-testid prefix collisions (8 elements match pcl-* but only 7 palette states expected).
Consider returning response_model with Pydantic for /api/training/nightly to avoid raw dict serialization surprises going forward.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py returns raw dict from /training/nightly; motor's insert_one mutates the dict with ObjectId. Using a Pydantic response_model or copying the dict before insert would prevent recurrence.
Chat endpoint creates a fresh LlmChat per request — OK, but re-sends up to 12 prior turns inline. If chat history grows, consider trimming by tokens not turns.
No rate limiting on /api/chat — could burn EMERGENT_LLM_KEY credits; fine for personal use but worth noting.
seed_data_creation: Used POST /api/state/reset at test start for deterministic seed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_gh05t3.py. Frontend uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Chat persisted in localStorage key 'gh05t3.session'. Identity header, TatorTot panel, 7 sub-agents, Memory Palace rooms, HCM 146/Feynman 59, PCL halo, Autotelic 21 goals, Scoreboard, KAIROS Run-Cycle and Nightly all verified working. The /training/nightly 500 bug was fixed in this iteration by excluding _id.
rca of the issue: POST /api/training/nightly 500: motor's insert_one mutates input dict by inserting ObjectId '_id'. The subsequent `return run` tried to serialize that ObjectId via FastAPI's jsonable_encoder which called vars(ObjectId) -> TypeError. Fix: `run.pop('_id', None)` before return (standard MongoDB ObjectId exclusion rule).
